title: Secondary workflow path
implementation_summary: Added the control-plane Claude review Secondary workflow path slice for RP00.P02.M04.S08.

The S08 policy, result-field registry, prechecker module, service function, validator, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README, and RP00 validator bind a frozen metadata-only secondary workflow result after RP00.P02.M04.S07.

The precheck consumes the S07 Primary happy path result and requires the S01 service entrypoint contract, S02 normalized request, S03 tenant boundary precheck, S04 Matter trace precheck, S05 Permission precheck, S06 Audit hint precheck, S07 Primary happy path result, RP00.P02.M03.S20 Hermes Validation denied-path evidence refs, and the permission/audit baseline RP00.P00.M05.S01 to remain intact.

The allowed path emits an allow_metadata_only secondary workflow receipt only when the carried S07 primary happy path decision is allow_metadata_only, the S07 result is marked applied, the entrypoint is claude_review, the model is claude-opus-4-8, effort is max, and read-only review metadata remains present.

Missing S07 results, wrong upstream markers, non-Claude-review entrypoints, wrong model or effort, non-read-only review metadata, missing receipts, missing audit refs, denied primary happy path results, secondary workflow bypass claims, audit ledger/event write claims, runtime Claude/review queue claims, runtime permission/AuthZ/permission-engine claims, review-gate bypass claims, unknown truthy secondary workflow claims, and source-completion claims fail closed.

The S08 result hard-codes no audit ledger append, no audit event write, no runtime Claude invocation, no review queue write, no review assignment write, no review notification, no service logic execution, no runtime route creation, no runtime permission evaluation, no AuthZ evaluator call, no permission engine execution, no product-state write, no database write, and no storage write.

The dedicated service tests prove policy binding, metadata-only secondary workflow receipt emission, firm-level metadata behavior, result drift rejection, unsafe-claim and wrong-source failures, prechecker no-write boundaries, S09 handoff, and synthetic fixture parity.

The RP00 validator now enforces S08 contract definition, policy module, service module, validators, fixture metadata, weighted-ledger presence, and construction inspection flag control_plane_claude_review_secondary_workflow_path_verified.

The boundary is metadata-only and does not append audit ledgers, write audit events, invoke Claude at runtime, mutate review queues, replace human approval, write storage, write databases, or persist product state.
RP00.P02.M04 remains open after S08 and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S09 State transition enforcement; RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
